free_text: Cross sections and isomeric cross-section ratios in the
interactions of fast neutrons with isotopes of mercury.
Data of Sub.010 were confirmed and used for comparison
The quasimonoenergetic neutrons were produced via
the H-2(d,n)He-3 reaction on a deuterium gas target
Effective neutron energy at each sample was calculated
using the Monte-Carlo program NEUT, see REL-REF
Stack target irradiation. Monitor foils of Al
and Fe were attached in front and at the back
At each energy, two irradiations were made - one with
the target filled with the deuterium gas and the other
Several samples, about 5 g of Hg-Cl2 powder of natural
isotopic composition, 99.9% purity, were pressed at
10 ton/cm**2 into a pellets 0.3 cm thick and 2.0 cm
Each pellet was placed in a Al-capsule, total
thickness of each sample including the capsule is
Monitor foils of Al, 200 micro-m thick, and Fe,
100 micro-m thick, of the same diameter as the capsule
The count rate of each gamma-ray was corrected for
coincidence summing effect, geometric effect of the
Hg-Cl2  sample and gamma-ray self-attenuation in the
sample. Corrections for interfering reactions, for
neutrons,for gamma-ray emission intensity and the
efficiency of the detector were made.
.The total uncertainty in each  cross section
values  was obtained by combining all the individual
uncertainties in quadrature,it amounted to about 20%
and was reported by authors in table 2.
Error in the averaging of the neutron flux is
Error in the efficiency of the gamma-ray
.EN-ERR  The error is reported by authors in table 2
The uncertainty in the excitation function
M.M. REL-REF C,2007NICE and BIB information
Ref.NSE,88,143,1984 moved to REL-REF; type of refs
mentioned in REL-REF modified. ERR-ANALYS update.
Of compiler. A misprint in Table II of main Reference
for En=11.25MeV was corrected to have right DATA
value 648.+-119.mb in according to comment of author
Energy error,the same as in SUBENTs .002,.003
The calculation of the exp. isomeric cross-section
ratio SIG-m/SIG-g for the isomeric pair Hg-195m,g  was
straightforward since both states decay independently,
and their formation cross sections were also determined
Energy error,the same as in SUBENTs .002,.003
The calculation of the exp. isomeric cross-section
ratio SIG-m/SIG-g for the isomeric pair Hg-195m,g  was
straightforward since both states decay independently,
and their formation cross sections were also determined
Data also on Fig.3 of REL-REF S.Sudar+C,2007NICE.
